text: There's this one scientific theory about why we haven't found aliens yet that's been keeping me up at night, so here it is. Okay, with over 100 billion Earth-like planets out there in the Milky Way, you think that we would have found some kind of life already, right? Well, scientists theorized that there's something called the Great Filter. Think of life as a series of filters, and each filter represents a barrier to advancing. For example, being in a solar system that is suitable for life as a filter. If there aren't the conditions for life, then life can't be lost. Being able to reproduce and then go from a single cell to a multicellar organism, that can be a filter too. If cells can't replicate, then you can't advance into larger animals. And of course, we as humans have overcome a lot of these filters to get where we are today. But one theory to why we haven't found aliens yet is that there's some great filter, some really big one that prevents species from becoming like us or further. So here's where it keeps me up at night. Scientists don't know if we passed the Great Filter already, or we're headed for it. More on this in my next video.
